name: Forest
note: die-number TODO from physical cards; starts with 1 stored (gold field starts 0)

name: Hills
note: die-number TODO from physical cards; starts with 1 stored (gold field starts 0)

name: Pasture
note: die-number TODO from physical cards; starts with 1 stored (gold field starts 0)

name: Fields
note: die-number TODO from physical cards; starts with 1 stored (gold field starts 0)

name: Mountains
note: die-number TODO from physical cards; starts with 1 stored (gold field starts 0)

name: Gold Field
note: die-number TODO from physical cards; starts with 1 stored (gold field starts 0)

name: Settlement
rules_text: Worth 1 victory point. Provides 2 building sites (above and below). Can be upgraded to a city.
unclear: cost is the canonical Catan/Rivals build cost — confirm against the physical card

name: City
rules_text: Upgrade of a settlement. Worth 2 victory points.
unclear: cost is the canonical Catan/Rivals city-upgrade cost — confirm against the physical card

name: Road
rules_text: Connects settlements and lets you build an additional settlement. That is its only function.
unclear: cost is the canonical Catan/Rivals build cost — confirm against the physical card

name: Toll Bridge
rules_text: Event Plentiful Harvest: You receive 2 gold.

name: Storehouse
rules_text: Do not count the resources on the 2 neighboring regions when the event Brigand Attack is rolled.

name: Iron Foundry
rules_text: Doubles the ore production of the neighboring mountains.

name: Grain Mill
rules_text: Doubles the grain production of the neighboring fields.

name: Lumber Camp
rules_text: Doubles the lumber production of the neighboring forests.

name: Brick Factory
rules_text: Doubles the brick production of the neighboring hills.

name: Weaver's Shop
rules_text: Doubles the wool production of the neighboring pastures.

name: ABBEY
rules_text: Provides 1 progress point (a prerequisite for the University).
flavor_text: Progress is not the only thing here; you also get red wine and lots of dark beer.
unclear: No effect text transcribed — confirm against the physical card whether it has printed rules or is a prerequisite-only card

name: MARKETPLACE
rules_text: If a production number is rolled that appears more frequently on your opponent's regions than on yours, you receive 1 resource. Choose a resource your opponent can normally receive.

name: PARISH HALL
rules_text: You pay only 1 resource for choosing a card from a draw stack.

name: LARGE TRADE SHIP
rules_text: You may trade 2 resources of the left or right neighboring region for any 1 other resource of your choice.

name: GOLD SHIP
rules_text: During your turn, you may trade 2 gold for any 1 other resource as often as you wish.

name: ORE SHIP
rules_text: During your turn, you may trade 2 ore for any 1 other resource as often as you wish.

name: Grain Ship
rules_text: During your turn, you may trade 2 grain for any 1 other resource as often as you wish.

name: Lumber Ship
rules_text: During your turn, you may trade 2 lumber for any 1 other resource as often as you wish.

name: Brick Ship
rules_text: During your turn, you may trade 2 brick for any 1 other resource as often as you wish.

name: Wool Ship
rules_text: During your turn, you may trade 2 wool for any 1 other resource as often as you wish.

name: Austin
flavor_text: If you hit my left cheek, don't even think you'll have time to hit the right one too.

name: Harald
flavor_text: I knock you out faster than you can carve the word "strategy" in this stone.

name: Inga
flavor_text: The gods are expecting more offerings. And they expect you to hand them over to me!

name: Osmund
flavor_text: When it comes to gold and women, friendship stops. And it also stops when it comes to power. Actually, friendship never lasts very long.

name: Candamir
flavor_text: A well-sharpened axe is a tried and tested starting position for a successful conversation.

name: Siglind
flavor_text: Turning men into heroes belittles the importance of being a hero.

name: Brigitta, the Wise Woman
rules_text: Play this card before rolling the dice. Choose the result of the production die roll.

name: Relocation
rules_text: You may exchange 2 of your own regions or 2 of your own expansion cards. Resources stored on regions may not be changed and card placement rules must be followed.

name: Scout
rules_text: Play this card when building a settlement. Take 2 cards of your choice from the region card stack. Reshuffle the region card stack.

name: Merchant Caravan
rules_text: Discard exactly 2 of your resources and take any 2 resources of your choice in return.

name: Goldsmith
rules_text: Discard 3 gold and take any 2 resources of your choice in return.

name: Invention
rules_text: Each player gets any 1 resource of his choice for each building with a progress point – up to a maximum of 2 resources.

name: Yule
rules_text: Shuffle the event card stack as performed at the beginning of the game. Afterwards, draw an event card again.

name: Year of Plenty
rules_text: Each region gets 1 resource for each adjacent Storehouse and Abbey, provided that storage space is available.

name: Fraternal Feuds
rules_text: The player who has the strength advantage selects 2 cards from the opponent's hand and returns them to the bottom of matching draw stacks.

name: Feud
rules_text: The player who has the strength advantage selects 3 of his opponent's buildings. The opponent must remove one of them and return it to the bottom of a matching draw stack.

name: Traveling Merchant
rules_text: Each player may take up to 2 resources of his choice, paying 1 gold per resource.

name: TRADE SHIPS RACE
rules_text: The player who owns the most trade ships receives any 1 resource of his choice. In case of a tie, each player receives any 1 resource of his choice (each must have at least 1 trade ship).

name: STOREHOUSE
rules_text: Do not count the resources on the 2 neighboring regions when the event Brigand Attack is rolled.

name: TOLL BRIDGE
rules_text: Event Plentiful Harvest: You receive 2 gold.

name: GOLD CACHE
rules_text: The Gold Cache may also be used to store the gold you received. When the event Brigand Attack is rolled, the gold in the cache is neither counted nor stolen. Requires: Hero with at least 1 strength point.

name: PIRATE SHIP
rules_text: Your opponent must remove 1 trade ship of his choice from his principality and place it on the discard pile. Event Plentiful Harvest: You receive 1 gold.

name: LARGE TRADE SHIP
rules_text: You may trade 2 resources of the left or right neighboring region for any 1 other resource of your choice.

name: MERCHANT GUILD

values.other: two balance-scale (commerce) symbols at bottom right

flavor_text: Money can't buy you happiness. But taking it away from others can.
unclear: values.commerce: the commerce point flag value and the two balance-scale symbol numbers are not legible enough to confirm exact counts

name: MONEYLENDER
rules_text: If you have the trade advantage and the event Trade is rolled on the event die, you may take 2 resources of your choice from your opponent.

name: HARBOR

values.note: worth +1 VP while >=3 trade ships in play

rules_text: As long as at least 3 trade ships are placed in your principality, the Harbor is worth 1 victory point.

name: TRADING BASE
rules_text: The Marketplace and the Harbor receive a second commerce point.

name: MINT
rules_text: Once per each of your turns, you may use this Mint to trade 1 gold for 1 other resource of your choice.

name: STAPLE HOUSE
rules_text: If you build the Staple House, you immediately receive 2 resources of your choice. Requires: Merchant Guild.

name: Salt Silo
rules_text: Each of your trade ships is worth 1 more commerce point.

name: Trade Master
rules_text: You immediately receive 2 resources of your choice from your opponent. Requires: Merchant Guild.

name: Merchant
rules_text: Take up to 2 resources of your choice from your opponent and give him 1 resource of your choice in return. Requires: 3 commerce points or city.

name: Reiner the Herald
rules_text: Play this card before rolling the dice and determine the event Celebration. You receive 1 additional resource for the Celebration.

name: Goldsmith
rules_text: Discard 3 gold and take any 2 resources of your choice in return.

name: Brigands
rules_text: You may take as many resources of the same type from your opponent as 1 of your regions can accommodate. Requires: Strength advantage.

name: Gudrun, Terror of the Seas
rules_text: For each of your Pirate Ships, your opponent must give you up to 2 gold.

name: Traveling Merchant
rules_text: Each player may take up to 2 resources of his choice. For each resource, 1 gold must be paid.

name: Trade Ships Race
rules_text: The player who owns the most trade ships receives any 1 resource of his choice. In case of a tie, each player receives any 1 resource of his choice (each must have at least 1 trade ship).

name: Gift for the Prince
rules_text: Each player receives 1 gold for each unit with at least 1 strength point.

name: Drill Ground
rules_text: Each hero you build in your principality costs you 1 resource of your choice less.

name: Lookout Tower
rules_text: When your opponent plays an Archer, Arsonist, or Traitor, roll the die. If you roll a 1 or 2, the card has no effect.

name: Carl Forkbeard
flavor_text: What a beautiful island! I take it.

name: Heinrich the Sentinel
rules_text: When your opponent plays an Archer, Arsonist, or Traitor, roll the die. If you roll a 3, 4, or 5, the card has no effect.

name: Irmgard, Keeper of the Light
rules_text: If you lose a card of your principality due to an event or an action, you receive any 1 resource of your choice.

name: Chapel
rules_text: If a 1, 2, or 3 is rolled with the production die, the event Riots does not apply to you.

name: Fairgrounds
rules_text: If you have more skill points than your opponent, you immediately receive 2 resources of your choice after building the Fairground.

name: Hedge Tavern (1x)
rules_text: Required to play Archer, Arsonist, and Traitor.
flavor_text: In the neighborhood, word has it that more people were seen going inside than coming out.
unclear: No effect text transcribed — confirm against the physical card whether it has printed rules or is a prerequisite-only card

name: Tithe Barn
rules_text: After building the Tithe Barn, you immediately receive either grain or wool – that is, one of the chosen resource for each of your heroes.

name: Large Festival Hall
rules_text: Worth 2 victory points.
flavor_text: Just a moment! Didn't the estimate specify a much bigger tower?
unclear: values: two red banner/flag icons at bottom-right – unsure what point value (e.g. victory_points or strength) they represent
cost: top icon row read as 2 gold, 2 ore, 2 brick but icon counts/types should be verified against physical card

name: Fire Brigade
rules_text: This city's buildings are safe from the Arsonist.

name: Voyage of Plunder
rules_text: If your opponent has more victory points, he must give you 2 resources of your choice. If he isn't in the lead, you only receive one resource. Requires: Strength advantage.

name: Archer
rules_text: Your opponent must place 1 of his own units with at least 1 strength point under a matching draw stack. Requires: Hedge Tavern.

name: Arsonist
rules_text: Choose 1 of your opponent's buildings adjacent to a settlement/city. He must place it under a draw stack of his choice. Requires: Hedge Tavern.

name: Brigands
rules_text: You may take as many resources of the same type from your opponent as 1 of your regions can accommodate. Requires: Strength advantage.

name: Sebastian the Itinerant Preacher
rules_text: If you play this card when the events Riots, Feud, or Fraternal Feud occur, these events do not apply to you.

name: Traitor
rules_text: Your opponent must show you all the cards in his hand. You may add 1 of them to your hand (in the Tournament Game, only units and action cards). Requires: Hedge Tavern.

name: Riots
rules_text: A player who has 1 or 2 units with strength points or commerce points pays 1 gold. A player who has more than 2 of these units pays 2 gold. If a player doesn't pay, he must remove one of these units and return it to the bottom of a matching draw stack.

name: Fraternal Feuds
rules_text: The player who has the strength advantage selects 2 cards from the opponent's hand and returns them to the bottom of matching draw stacks.

name: Feud
rules_text: The player who has the strength advantage selects 3 of his opponent's buildings. The opponent must remove one of them and return it to the bottom of a matching draw stack.

name: Chief Cannoneer
rules_text: Requires: University.
flavor_text: I'll show you how to produce a romantic castle ruin.

name: TOWN HALL
rules_text: Place the Town Hall on your Parish Hall. At the end of your turn, you no longer pay for choosing a card.

name: UNIVERSITY
rules_text: Requires: Abbey or Library.
flavor_text: Formerly, there was a hedge-tavern here. Now there are two of them next door.

name: LIBRARY
rules_text: When you build the Library, you may immediately choose a card from a draw stack (Tournament: from your own stack).

name: PHARMACY
rules_text: When the event Plague occurs, you receive any 1 resource of your choice. You receive this resource whether you previously lost resources or not.

name: BATH HOUSE
rules_text: Protects all 4 regions bordering this city from the effects of the event Plague.

name: BUILDING CRANE
rules_text: Every city expansion you build that costs more than 4 resources costs you 1 resource less. Requires: University.

name: PARLIAMENT

values.other: two region-card/road symbols shown at right of title (orange settlement icons)

rules_text: For the benefit of the people and their representatives in particular. Requires: 2 progress points.
flavor_text: For the benefit of the people and their representatives in particular.

name: THREE-FIELD SYSTEM

values.other: A (action card letter, top-left in shield)

rules_text: You receive up to 2 grain. Requires: University.

name: MINERAL MINING

values.other: A (action card letter, top-left in shield)

rules_text: You receive up to 2 ore. Requires: University.

name: Benjamin the Traveling Scholar
rules_text: You once more receive the resource of each region whose number you rolled at the beginning of your turn.

name: Guido the Ambassador
rules_text: You may choose 1 card from the discard pile (Tournament: from your opponent's discard pile). Requires: Town Hall or fewer victory points than your opponent.

name: Gustav the Librarian
rules_text: You may choose 1 card from the discard pile (Tournament: from your own discard pile). Requires: Library or fewer victory points than your opponent.

name: Doctor
rules_text: Each region bordering your Bath House receives 1 resource. If you have various Bath Houses, you may only use 1 of them.

name: Brigitta, the Wise Woman
rules_text: Play this card before rolling the dice. Choose the result of the production die roll.

name: Relocation
rules_text: You may exchange 2 of your own regions or 2 of your own expansion cards. Resources stored on regions may not be changed and card placement rules must be followed.

name: Plague
rules_text: Every region bordering a city loses 1 resource.

name: INVENTION
rules_text: Each player gets any 1 resource of his choice for each building with a progress point – up to a maximum of 2 resources.

name: Pilgrimage Site
rules_text: If by means of an event or an action played by your opponent—you lose any of the cards in your hand, draw as many cards from any of the draw stacks from the new era as you would have lost.

name: Great Thingstead
rules_text: Both players discard 1 card each from their hand. The event Religious Dispute no longer affects any of the players.

name: Odin's Fountain
rules_text: At the head of your turn, instead of drawing 2 cards, draw 4 cards instead of 1. If you choose the cards, each time instead of you must pay the respective costs.

name: Reiner the Miller
rules_text: Place adjacent to a field region. You may trade grain of this field's region for resources of different types at a 2:1 rate. The field must still be in your field region. Trading your turns you may also trade at a 1:1 rate.

name: Abbey Brewery
rules_text: Place adjacent to a region for every 2 grain from the adjacent fields region, you may rotate the Abbey Brewery to any side of your building. Requires: City.

name: Red Light Tavern
rules_text: Place on a forest of an adjacent settlement/city, for each unit featuring strength points in the settlement, all other properties remain unchanged. Removal: The opponent has at least 3 fortifies.

name: Judith, Guardian of the Church
rules_text: Place on your Church. Your Church is protected against all action cards, effects of expansion cards, and events. In during your turn, you receive up to 2 gold.

name: Bran, Defender of the Temple
rules_text: Place on your Temple. Draw immediately 2 cards from the draw stacks (from your own draw stacks). Your Temple is protected against effects of expansion cards, and events.

name: Master of the Brotherhood
rules_text: If one opponent wants to play a facet fact that could cause the loss of 1 card of your land or 1 of the cards you placed, he first must pay 2 gold to each opponent. If you do not pay the required gold the opponent keeps your facet land. You may use this with Requires: not having an Intrigue.

name: Godfrey the Intriguer
rules_text: If you build Godfrey, during your next turn you may pay 1 gold to view the cards in your opponent's hand and take 1 card of your choice from it. Place Godfrey on the discard pile (on your own discard pile).

name: Church
rules_text: After building the Church, you immediately receive up to 2 gold.

name: Odin's Temple
rules_text: After building the Temple, immediately draw 1 card from any draw stack (from any of your own draw stacks).

name: Sacrificial Site
rules_text: You may trade wool for a resource of a different type at a 2:1 rate. Wolf from neighboring pasture regions if another opponent can be found. Attack occurs.

name: Bishop's See
rules_text: If you build the Bishop's See, you may choose 3 of your opponent's units and put them on the discard pile (on his own discard pile).

name: Missionary
rules_text: Your opponent must remove any 1 of the heroes he placed. Place the removed hero in your hand and put one of your own discard pile.

name: Bishop
rules_text: Demand 1 gold from your opponent. In addition, you receive any cards in buildings he can accomplish in any gold fields or in the Cold Castle.

name: Odin's Priest
rules_text: Your opponent must show you the card in his hand and place it on 1 of your stacks (under his own matching stacks).

name: Priestess of the Horns
rules_text: Choose 1 draw stack and select up to 2 cards from it.

name: Michael the Master Builder
rules_text: Play this card when you build a building. Substitute 1, 2, or 3 of the resources required for building with 1 gold each.

name: Good Neighbors
rules_text: Reveal the top cards of all draw stacks. The player who has the more cards wins. That player takes 1 draw stack from 1 of the other draw stacks into his hand.

name: Religious Dispute
rules_text: Players who have at least 1 city place all cards in their hands under the matching expansion card stacks. The Church and the temple each decrease two 2+p. If the result matches an expansion card stack, if you receive it 1 card. If you have a Bishop's See, you receive up to 3 gold.

name: Wainwright
rules_text: 1x during your turn: Take 1 resource or trade between two regions of the same type or trade 2 resources of your choice for 1 different resource.

name: Cloth Merchant's Residence
rules_text: Place this building card from the adjacent pasture region, you may rotate the Residence to the next higher level. Requires: City.

name: Paper Merchant's Residence
rules_text: Place this building card from the adjacent forest region, you may rotate the Residence to the next higher level. Requires: City.

name: Trading Post
rules_text: 1x during your turn: You may trade 1 resource between the two adjacent regions at a 1:1 ratio.

name: Brigand Camp
rules_text: Place on a free road of your opponent. Your opponent's commerce points are reduced by 1. If your opponent is robbed in this location, share 1 resource, you receive 1 gold.

name: Ship Builder
rules_text: Each ship you build costs you either 1 lumber or 1 wool less. For 1 gold, you may take 1 trade ship from the discard pile (from your own discard pile).

name: Olaf the Merchant Ship Captain
rules_text: During one of your following turns, you may disband 1 or 2 resources from your opponent and give him any 1 resource of your choice. Afterwards, place Olaf on the discard pile (on your own discard pile).

name: Pirate Ship
rules_text: Your opponent must remove 1 trade ship of his choice from the discard pile (on his own discard pile). Event: Plaintiff finished, you receive 1 gold.

name: Commercial Metropolis

values.requires: 1 Residence of the highest level or 6 commerce points

rules_text: Place on a 'City' card.

name: Trading Station
rules_text: Place on a building site of one of your opponent's cities. 1x during your turn, you may take 1 resource of your choice from your opponent. Choose from your opponent.

name: Commercial Harbor
rules_text: 1x during your turn, take discard fish. If all Residences by 1 level and take any 2 resources of your choice.

name: Lighthouse

values.requires: Commercial Harbor or at least 2 sea ships

rules_text: 1x during your turn, 1 sea ship directly adjacent to the Lighthouse, you may use this ship to trade at 1:1 rate. If you build a house on the Lighthouse level and you must discard sea harbor. Requires: Commercial Harbor or at least 2 sea ships.

name: Craft Guild
rules_text: After building the Craft Guild, rotate each of your Residences to the next higher level. If you can't find a Residence, you must rotate any 2 Residences to the next higher level instead.

name: Master Merchants' Alliance
rules_text: Each time your opponent trades 2 or 3 resources of one type for a different resource in any monopoly, you increase by 1 resource of the type your opponent just received. If it is not 1 resource of the type your opponent paid during this trade.

name: Guild Master
rules_text: Take up to 2 resources of your choice.

name: Herold the Master Merchant

values.requires: Commercial Harbor or at least 2 trade ships

rules_text: During the turn you play Herold, you may trade a resource card at home as if you like.

name: Gero the Master Merchant
rules_text: Take any 1 or 2 resources of your choice.

name: Tactical Retreat
rules_text: If you remove one of the buildings you placed in your opponent's principality, you receive any 2 resources of your choice. 1 of which your opponent must give you.

name: Mendicants
rules_text: If your opponent has the trade advantage, demand 1 resource from him. If he also has more victory points than you, demand up to 2 resources from him.

name: Trade Monopoly
rules_text: Demand 1, 2, of 3 resources of one type from your opponent and give him 1 resource of your choice in return.

name: Maritime Trade Monopoly
rules_text: For each trade ship you have in excess of your opponent's trade ship total, demand 1 resource from him—a maximum of 2 resources.

name: Fortunate Trade Voyage
rules_text: For 1 of the trade ships, each player receives up to 2 resources of the type traded by the ship. Large Trade Ship: Each player receives 2 resources of the left or right region.

name: Capricious Sea
rules_text: Calm Sea (1, 2, 3): At the end of the turn, all trade ships; each player receives 1 resource instead. Place it in his propensity under a matching draw stack of his own.

name: Hour of the Master Merchants
rules_text: Rotate each coin Residences to the next higher level. If 1 or more Residences already reached level 3, each region adjacent to these Residences receives 1 resource instead.

name: White Raven Tavern
rules_text: 1x during your turn, draw a Development card. If a '3', '4', or '5' is rolled, you do not receive any resources, if a '4', '5', '6' is rolled, you receive 1 resource; if a '6' is rolled, you receive 2 resources.

name: Border Fortress
rules_text: Place adjacent to a hills region where at least 1 brick is stored. For 1 hand you may rotate the Border Fortress to the next higher level.

name: Triumph Card
rules_text: Place adjacent to any region of your choice. If you come out victorious after a Barbarian Attack, instead of taking 2 resources, you may play the Triumph Card to the next higher level.

name: Barbarian Stronghold
rules_text: Place a free road adjacent to one of your opponent's cities. For your opponent, the strength of the Barbarian Stronghold indicates 3 victory points.

name: Arad the Strategist
rules_text: When a Barbarian Attack occurs, each hero in the settlement/city where a road is placed counts double—except for Arad himself.

name: Baroc the Barbarian
rules_text: Place adjacent to any region where a Barbarian Stronghold is in your opponent's principality. You receive 2 ore.

name: Siward the Scout
rules_text: 1x during your turn: You may draw a top card of the event card stack (of any draw stack (of any draw stack)). If you may pay 1 gold to view the cards in your opponent's hand.

name: Wolfgang the Street Performer
rules_text: Each time a player places a building, it is adjacent to Wolfgang. If you take something, your opponent may place Wolfgang in the principality for 2 gold for the talesman.

name: Caravel
rules_text: Each ship in the settlement/city where the Caravel is placed counts double in case of a Caravel itself. Requires: City.

name: Marie the Shieldmaiden
rules_text: During one of your action turns, you may demand that your opponent choose 2 of his heroes that can be placed on Marie's building. Place a token on the discard pile (on your own discard pile).

name: Secret Brotherhood
rules_text: 1) If you lose against the barbarians, you only have to pay 1 resource per turn from your hand. If you do not an unplayed unit of coin card, you receive any 1 resource of your choice.

name: Bailwick
rules_text: 1x during your turns, you may draw the top card from a draw stack (from your own draw stack).

name: Castle
rules_text: In case of a Barbarian Attack, add strength point of your Border Fortress counts as 1 additional unit.

name: Arsenal
rules_text: In case of a Brigand Attack, the resources of the region adjacent to the Arsenal neither counted nor stolen.

name: Siegfried, Vanquisher of the Barbarians
rules_text: Triumph Card to the next higher level.

name: Alliance Against the Barbarians

values.requires: Triumph Card indicating at least 1 victory point, plus at least 1 unit.

rules_text: Each player who has at least 1 unit takes 1 resource of his choice or rotates your Triumph Card. Each player with remaining resources must rotate a card. Requires: Triumph Card indicating at least 1 victory point, plus at least 1 unit.

name: Castellan
rules_text: The two regions adjacent to your Castle each receive 1 resource—if storage space is available.

name: Relocation
rules_text: Swap 2 of your own regions or 2 of your own expansion cards. The number of resources or development cards and the placement of the cards remain unchanged, and the placement of the cards remain compliant with the rules.

name: Contest of the Heroes
rules_text: Determine 1 of your own heroes and 1 hero of your opponent (if he has one). For each hero: Roll one die. The hero with the highest strength points wins. In case of a tie, roll the die again. The winner receives 1 resource and may discard another. If may discard another, the loser receives from the bank.

name: Barbarian Attack
rules_text: A player who has the most units than any other player gains 1 victory point. Otherwise: He loses units and third units lost in the cities. He loses units and at least 1 city receives 2 resources. Afterwards: The card is placed in the discard pile and a top card is drawn again.

name: Retreat of the Barbarians
rules_text: A player who has at least 1 unit loses 1 card from their hand. Then a player with units has at least 1 unit and the strength advantage divines up to 2 cards from 1 new stack (from 1 of two draw stacks).

name: Shipwreck
rules_text: Discovery: You steal any 1 resource of your choice. Missions to the Shipwreck are not possible.

name: Island of the Bards
rules_text: Discovery: Your opponent shows you 2 cards from any 1 of their own draw piles. You may draw 1 action card or unit into your hand. Mission: Pay 2 grain to raise the card to the next higher level.

name: Island of the Merchants
rules_text: Discovery: You may immediately trade any 1 resource of your choice for any 1 other resource of your choice. Mission: Pay 2 wool to rotate the card to the next higher level.

name: Islands of the Scholars
rules_text: Discovery: You may draw up to 2 cards from any 1 of your own draw piles. You may draw 1 action card or unit into your hand. Mission: Pay 2 lumber to rotate the card to the next higher level.

name: Zheng He
rules_text: Discovery/Mission: Pay 1 gold or fight. Fight: Roll the production die and add the bounty. Defeat: You lose the card and this card turns over to its blank side faces up). Tie: Roll the production die again!

name: Cinmarone
rules_text: Discovery/Mission: Pay 1 gold or fight. Fight: Roll the production die and add the bounty. Defeat: You lose the card and this card turns from the taken card side to its blank side faces up). Tie: Roll the production die again!

name: Jean
rules_text: Discovery/Mission: Pay 2 resources or fight. Fight: Roll the production die and add the bounty. Defeat: You lose the card and this card turns from the taken card side to its blank side faces up). Tie: Roll the production die again!

name: Island of the Forgotten Tribe
rules_text: Discovery: Return the ship to the Explorer Harbor. You may carry out an additional mission. Mission: Roll a die!—you lose your ship (turn the disc token over so the sail side faces up). Tie an event. 5-6: Treasure—rotate the card to the next higher level.

name: Explorer Harbor
rules_text: Building an explorer ship (turn the disc token over so the sail side is visible). You may only make 1 discovery during each of your turns.

values.requires: Place on the city adjacent to the Explorer Harbor. Requires: At least 2 level 3 lands.

name: Armory
rules_text: No more diplomas understanding! People understand our language everywhere.

name: Sailmakers' Shop
rules_text: Should you experience prolonged calms — we also sell rum.

name: Landing Stage
rules_text: Place above or below a region directly adjacent to your Explorer Harbor. 1x during your turn: On a mission to the island of the gods, sailed by a previous tribe or merchants, you may replace 1 of the resources you have to pay with 1 gold.

name: Shipyard
rules_text: Place above or below a region directly adjacent to your Explorer Harbor. 1x during your turn: You may trade any 1 resource of your choice for 1 lumber or 1 wool.

name: Astronomer
rules_text: 1x during your turn: If you have discovered fewer sea cards than your opponent, you may add to your second journey of exploration.

name: Cannon Foundry
rules_text: 1x during your turn: If you roll a pirate, you may roll against a pirate, you may repeat the die roll once.

name: Cartographer
rules_text: View up to 2 of your face-down sea cards and/or switch the positions of any 2 sea cards of your choice in your principality.

name: Broadside
rules_text: Your opponent must either rotate 1 blank card to the next lower level or turn 1 explorer ship over (so that its blank side faces up) — you choose.

name: Lars the Naval Hero
rules_text: When you fight against a pirate, the die roll is omitted and the pirate is defeated. Requires: At least 1 cannon point.

name: Navigator
rules_text: Return 1 of your ships you have used for an action to your Explorer Harbor. You may perform another action with this ship. Requires: 2 sail points.

name: Ambassador

values.requires: Requires: You have leveled the Bland of the Burds, the Bland of the Scholars, or the Bland of the Merchants another time

rules_text: You get the advantage of discovering the Bland of the Burds, the Bland of the Scholars, or the Bland of the Merchants another time. Requires: You have leveled the Bland of the Burds, the Bland of the Scholars, or the Bland of the Merchants another time.

name: Friendship Between Peoples
rules_text: Each player receives any 1 resource of his choice for each blind card he has rotated to the next level.

name: Most Successful Explorer
rules_text: The player who discovered the most sea cards in his personality (at least 1) may draw up to 2 cards from any 1 draw stack of his choice (normally 1 draw stack of his choice). The cards he draws are added to his hand. The player whose turn it is begins.

name: Grove of Freedom
rules_text: 1x during your turn: You may draw 1 card from a draw stack (then one of your own draw stacks).

name: Grove of Fraternity
rules_text: 1x during your turn: You may draw 3 cards from any of your own draw stacks of your choice. Afterwards, your opponent may draw from any one of his own draw stacks of his choice (from any of his own draw stacks of his choice).

name: Grove of Justice
rules_text: Use this card before your die roll and disqualify the result of the event die roll.

name: Grove of Peace
rules_text: Roll the production die if your opponent plays an action attack card. If the roll result is larger than 3, the card has no effect and you discard it on the discard pile.

name: Grove of Vigilance
rules_text: If the event Trade, Colonisation, or Brigand Attack is rolled, you may replace the event. Instead, the event Event Card applies.

name: Grove of Great Foresight
rules_text: 1x during your turn: You may view the top 3 topmost event cards. You may place as many of these event cards under the stack as you like, except for the rule card.

name: Grove of Courage
rules_text: 1x during your own: you were to lose cards from your hand or buildings due to a Brigand attack, these event cards would have no effect.

name: Manifest of Humane Conduct
rules_text: Pay 1 lumber, 2 gold, and 3 owls to activate the card to the next hand. For 2 of the owls you have to pay, you also may place 1 action attack card on the discard pile.

name: Peter, Sage of the Forest
rules_text: Place adjacent to a forest region with at least 1 lumber. Pay this lumber. If the number of the adjoining forest regions is rolled during the production roll, rotate Peter to the next higher level.

name: Michaela, Sage of the Pasture
rules_text: Place adjacent to a pasture region with at least 1 wool. Pay this wool. If the number of the adjoining pasture regions is rolled during the production roll, rotate Michaela to the next higher level.

name: Barbara, Sage of the Fields
rules_text: Place adjacent to a fields region with at least 1 grain. Pay this grain. If the number of the adjoining fields region is rolled during the production roll, rotate Barbara to the next higher level.

name: Frederich, Sage of the Hills
rules_text: Place adjacent to a hills region with at least 1 brick. Pay this brick. If the number of the adjoining hills region is rolled during the production roll, rotate Frederich to the next higher level.

name: Piet, Sage of the Mountains
rules_text: Place adjacent to a mountains region with at least 1 ore. Pay this ore. If the number of the adjoining mountains region is rolled during the production roll, rotate Piet to the next higher level.

name: Walther, Sage of the Gold Field
rules_text: Place adjacent to a gold field region with at least 1 gold. Pay this gold. If the number of the adjoining gold field region is rolled during the production roll, rotate Walther to the next higher level.

name: Principal Sage Woman
rules_text: Place adjacent to any region of your choice with at least 1 resource. Pay this resource. If the number of the adjoining region is rolled during the production roll, rotate Principal Sage Woman to the next higher level.

name: Robert, Herald of the Sages
rules_text: Each time you must roll the production die to surpass either a strength value or a card of the owls rolled by your opponent, you win also in case of a tie.

name: Cole, Paladin of the Sages
rules_text: 1x during your turn: You may use the function of one of your owls. You may pay the same function on the number of owls indicated on that glove.

name: Granary
rules_text: 1x during your turn: If enemy regions has no grain, you may give him 1 grain, which he must use in that region. Afterwards, any 1 resource of a different type of your choice from him.

name: Academy of Sages
rules_text: 1x during your turn: If you need 1 or more of your choice and rotate any 1 sage card to the next higher level.

name: Courthouse
rules_text: If you lose resources when the event trade is rolled, you determine which resources to give to your opponent.

name: Great Foresight
rules_text: View the event card stack and remove — without changing the order of the cards — 1 event (except for the Rule).

name: Dispute of the Sages

values.requires: Both players each have at least 1 sage.

rules_text: Pay 1 owl: You and your opponent roll the production die, and each adds his sages' owls to his result. The winner receives any 2 resources of his choice. The other receives 1 resource.

name: Wise Compensation

values.requires: Fewer cities or victory points than your opponent.

rules_text: Pay 3 owls: You may build 1 city for free (without paying resources).

name: Power of the Groves
rules_text: You receive 1 owl for each grove in your principality. You may distribute the owls among your sages as desired.

name: Wise Protection
rules_text: Pay 1 owl: Your opponent must show you his hand of cards. If it contains an action attack card, he must place all cards in his hand face down and you may draw 1 card (under any matching draw stacks of his own).

name: Famine
rules_text: Each player without a grain must discard 1 grain. If a player doesn't have grain, he must discard 2 resources of his choice.

name: Council of the Sages
rules_text: Each player may distribute, at his own discretion, either up to 2 swiqs among his sages or up to 2 progress among regions with an adjacent logic.

name: Common Land
rules_text: 1 x during your turn: You may trade any 1 resource of your choice for 1 different resource.

name: Village School
rules_text: After building the Village School, you immediately receive up to 2 swiqs. You also immediately take back 1 star each time the event Trade occurs.

name: Traveling Theater
rules_text: Place on one of your own free roads. You receive 1 star. 1x during your turn: Move the traveling Theater onto one of your own free roads. Trade 1 resource of an adjacent region for 1 star.

name: Mercenaries
rules_text: Place on one of your own free roads. You may keep 1 gold and 1 year beyond Afrond. If you pay 1 star, you keep all resources.

name: Small Market Town
rules_text: Place on one of your own free roads. 1x during your turn: If one of your regions has 3 resources, you may trade these resources for any 1 different resource.

name: Public Feeling
rules_text: If you pay 1 star, the events Riot and Insurrection do not affect you. 1x during your turn: For 1 star, you receive any 1 resource of your choice.

name: Thieves' Hideout
rules_text: Place adjacent to a region of your opponent's. During your turn: For 1 wool, you may place the Thieves adjacent to another region of your opponent. The placed region must have at least 5 strength points or pay 3 discs.

name: Prince
rules_text: 1x during your turn: 1 gold, you may retrieve 1 artwork from the discard pile (from your own discard piles). The princess never cover to the opponent's princess or to the cards in his hand.

name: Princess
rules_text: 1x during your turn: 1 gold, you may retrieve 1 artwork from the discard pile (from your own discard piles). The princess never cover to the opponent's princess or to the cards in his hand.

name: Aqueduct
rules_text: If the number of a field or pasture regions adjacent to the city with the Aqueduct is tripled, you receive 1 additional resource there.

name: Hospital
rules_text: 1x during your turn: You receive 1 star, or you pay 1 star and receive any 1 resource of your choice in exchange.

name: Theater
rules_text: After building the Theater, you immediately receive 1 star.

name: Monument to the Prince
rules_text: After you have built the Monument, your opponent must determine 2 knight cards placed. Choose 1 of them and add it to your hand.

name: City Palace
rules_text: 1x during your turn: Pay 3 stars and draw 1 action card. This action does not count as a requirement for an action from the discard pile (from your own discard pile) into your hand.

name: Builder's Hut
rules_text: (1) If the event Colonization is ruled, you receive 1 star. (2) A building that costs 1 less resource of your choice costs you 1 resource less.

name: Feeding the Poor
rules_text: Pay 1 grain. You receive up to 2 stars.

name: Artwork: Sculpture
rules_text: You receive 1 star. If you have a Builder's Hut, you receive another 1 star.

name: Artwork: Epic
rules_text: You receive 1 star. If you have a Theater, you receive another 1 star.

name: Artwork: Fountain
rules_text: You receive 1 star. If you have an Aqueduct, you receive another 1 star.

name: Artwork: Relief
rules_text: You receive 1 star. If you have a City Palace, you receive another 1 star.

name: Court Astrologer
rules_text: If you play this card and pay 1 star, you may once more determine an event die and put it into your turn.

name: Bera the Insurrectionist
rules_text: You choose: Your opponent either gives you 2 stars, or he gives you 1 resource of his choice for each victory point he has, to a maximum of 3 resources.

name: Prosperity
rules_text: Building a city costs you only 1 ore and 2 grain.

name: Insurrection
rules_text: Each player has to remove 1 of his own buildings that at least costs 2 resources, and place it under a matching draw stack (under one of its resources). Afterwards: The Insurrection is placed under the 4 top cards of the event card stack.

name: Taxation
rules_text: Each player who pays 1 star receives any 1 resource of his choice and 1 gold.
